name: Kansas Dietitian License
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Kansas - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Health Occupations Credentialing
provider.parentOrganization: Kansas Department for Aging and Disability Services (KDADS)

address.description:
Kansas Board of Healing Arts
800 SW Jackson, Lower Level - Suite A
Topeka, KS 66612

requiredDocuments.name: Notarized Application
requiredDocuments.description: The original downloaded application form with a notarized signature.

requiredDocuments.name: CDR Registration Card
requiredDocuments.description: A copy of your active CDR card to document exam and training completion.

requiredDocuments.name: Official College Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from the university showing your conferred degree.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Kansas dietitian license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial license application fee is $140. A temporary license costs $70.

mainEntity.name: When does the Kansas license need to be renewed?

acceptedAnswer.text: Kansas regular dietitian licenses are renewed biennially (every two years). The online renewal system opens annually on January 15th for those due. The renewal fee is $135.

mainEntity.name: What are the continuing education requirements for Kansas?

acceptedAnswer.text: Kansas requires a minimum of 15 hours of Continuing Professional Education (CPE) every two years. Holding current CDR registration (which requires 75 CPEs every 5 years) generally satisfies this requirement.

mainEntity.name: Does Kansas have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Kansas. To work in Kansas, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Kansas?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 3 to 4 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Kansas while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
